fields.label: Authentication Provider
fields.description: Specify which AWS credentials chain to use.

fields.label: Credentials Profile Name
fields.description: Credentials profile name, as specified in ~/.aws/credentials, leave blank for default.

fields.label: Assume Role ARN
fields.description:
Optional. Specifying the ARN of a role will ensure that the
                     selected authentication provider is used to assume the role rather than the
                     credentials directly.

validations.message: When set, must be a valid IAM role ARN, e.g. arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/MyRole

fields.label: External ID
fields.description: If you are assuming a role in another account, that has been created with an external ID, specify the external ID here.

fields.label: Endpoint
fields.description: Optionally, specify a custom endpoint for the service

fields.label: Default Region
fields.description: Specify the region, such as for US West (Oregon) use ` us-west-2 ` as the region.

relationships.description: AWS access key pair — supplied together when authType is 'keys'
STS assume-role parameters — external ID is only meaningful when assumeRoleArn is set

instructions.msg: Six authentication values are stored in jsonData.authType. The five editor-selectable ones are: 'default' (AWS SDK Default — the default credential chain), 'keys' (Access & secret key — puts accessKey/secretKey in secureJsonData), 'credentials' (Credentials file — optionally provide jsonData.profile), 'ec2_iam_role' (Workspace IAM Role — no extra credentials needed), and 'grafana_assume_role' (Grafana Assume Role — Grafana Cloud only; visible only when the awsDatasourcesTempCredentials feature toggle is on and grafana-x-ray-datasource is in the ConnectionConfig allow-list, which it is). The sixth is 'arn' (deprecated legacy value; the backend maps it to 'default').

Minimal payload per auth method (jsonData.defaultRegion is required in practice — the backend copies it into Region when Region is empty; queries need a region to build the AWS X-Ray / Application Signals client). Access & secret key: {"jsonData":{"authType":"keys","defaultRegion":"us-east-1"},"secureJsonData":{"accessKey":"<AKID>","secretKey":"<SECRET>"}}. Credentials file: {"jsonData":{"authType":"credentials","profile":"my-profile","defaultRegion":"us-east-1"}}. Workspace IAM Role: {"jsonData":{"authType":"ec2_iam_role","defaultRegion":"us-east-1"}}. AWS SDK Default: {"jsonData":{"authType":"default","defaultRegion":"us-east-1"}}. Grafana Assume Role: {"jsonData":{"authType":"grafana_assume_role","defaultRegion":"us-east-1"}}.

Legacy auth interpretation: a provisioned config with jsonData.authType == 'arn' is treated as 'default' by the backend (awsds.AuthType.UnmarshalJSON, awsds/settings.go:87-88). A provisioned config with authType == 'sharedCreds' is normalized to 'credentials' the same way but is NOT in this schema's allowedValues — the schema will reject it even though the backend still loads it. Additionally: if jsonData.profile is empty, the X-Ray plugin's getDsSettings (pkg/datasource/configuration.go:16-18) falls back to the root-level datasource `database` field as a legacy profile. Preserve that root-level `database` value on round-trip when it is set.

Session token: secureJsonData.sessionToken is written by provisioning only (the ConnectionConfig editor never renders an input for it). The backend (awsds/settings.go:137) still reads it, so pair it with authType == 'keys' for temporary AWS STS credentials — {"jsonData":{"authType":"keys","defaultRegion":"us-east-1"},"secureJsonData":{"accessKey":"<AKID>","secretKey":"<SECRET>","sessionToken":"<STS-TOKEN>"}} — or with authType == 'grafana_assume_role' (added in plugin v2.17.0 to support Grafana Assume Role's temporary credentials).

secureJsonData values (accessKey, secretKey, sessionToken) are write-only: they can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether a secret is already configured on an existing datasource, read the secureJsonFields object (e.g. secureJsonFields.accessKey == true) instead of secureJsonData. Backend-only secrets like sessionToken never surface in secureJsonFields until they are set.

Connection quirks: this plugin has no plugin-specific jsonData fields — its ConfigEditor (src/components/ConfigEditor/ConfigEditor.tsx:11-19) renders only the @grafana/aws-sdk ConnectionConfig plus the SecureSocksProxy toggle (deliberately excluded from this schema). The 'AWS Application Signals' product name comes from a rename (plugin v2.16.0); the plugin id and directory are still 'grafana-x-ray-datasource' for backward compatibility. There is no per-request tracking flag or plugin-specific endpoint override on top of what ConnectionConfig already exposes.
